operating_state: Net sales increased 8.1% to $41.1 billion driven by 4.5% comparable sales growth reflecting 11.3% higher average ticket offset by 6.0% fewer customer transactions. Average ticket increases resulted from commodity price inflation, particularly lumber, and increased big-ticket transactions. Ten of fourteen merchandising departments posted positive comparable sales, led by Kitchen and Bath and Lumber, while Paint, Hardware, and Garden departments experienced single-digit declines.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin compressed to 33.2% from 34.0% primarily driven by lumber rate and mix effects, rising transportation costs, and One Supply Chain investments. SG&A expenses decreased as a percentage of sales to 15.6% from 16.7%, benefiting from sales leverage and cycling prior year COVID-19 expenses, partially offset by increased hourly payroll costs from higher demand and wage investments.
material_events: The company repurchased $6.9 billion of common stock in the first six months after resuming buybacks following March 2020 suspension. Quarterly dividend increased 10% to $1.65 per share in February 2021. The company fully repaid senior notes in March 2021 and completed a $3.0 billion debt offering in September 2021 with notes due 2028, 2031, and 2051.
macro_exposures: Commodity price inflation, particularly lumber, pressured both product mix and margin performance while rising transportation costs impacted gross margins. A weaker U.S. dollar provided $385 million positive impact to sales growth during the quarter.
forward_outlook: Management expects to maintain capital expenditures at approximately two percent of net sales annually while following disciplined capital allocation. The company may maintain elevated cash positions during fiscal 2021 and adjust capital expenditures to support operations or respond to economic conditions as necessary.
uncertainty_profile: The company maintains commercial paper programs allowing borrowings up to $3.0 billion with backup credit facilities totaling $3.0 billion. Working capital remains subject to seasonality, inventory management, vendor payment timing, and foreign exchange rate fluctuations. No material changes were reported to critical accounting policies or risk factors.
